summary: Renamed several Kirby translation units and their public include names from ftKb_* style to lowercase ftkirby/ftkirbyspecial* paths, then propagated those path changes through splits, configure.py object lists, Kirby/common fighter sources, item sources, and player/fighter support files. The slice shows mostly rename/include maintenance plus minor formatting-only edits; it contains no PR body explanation and no review comments.

key_files.role: Updated split entries for renamed Kirby TUs while preserving section ranges shown in the diff.

Renamed ftKirby TU/split/config paths: ftKb_Init.c to ftkirby.c, ftKb_SpecialN.c to ftkirbyspecialn.c, ftKb_SpecialNKp.c to ftkirbyspecialkoopa.c, ftKb_SpecialNLk.c to ftkirbyspeciallink.c, ftKb_SpecialNSs.c to ftkirbyspecialsamus.c, and ftKb_SpecialNYs.c to ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c.
Renamed ftKb_Init.h to ftkirby.h and ftKb_Init.dox to ftkirby.dox according to the diff excerpt.
Updated includes throughout touched fighter/common/item/player files from ftKb_Init.h include paths to ftkirby.h include paths.
Updated a comment in ftkirbyspecialn.c from 'External variables from ftKb_Init.c' to 'External variables from ftkirby.c'.
Included minor formatting-only changes visible in ftCo_Attack100.c and ft_0C31.c, such as brace/line wrapping and spacing in a function declaration.
smart_moves: The rename was propagated through both build/config surfaces and source include sites rather than only renaming files.
The split file entries were updated at the same section ranges as the old names, preserving the split boundaries shown in the diff.
configure.py kept the existing Matching versus NonMatching status for each renamed object path shown in the diff, avoiding unsupported status changes.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When renaming translation units in this repository, update all path-bearing surfaces together: split metadata, configure object lists, file names, headers/docs, and include sites.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: config/GALE01/splits.txt path updates
diff_excerpt: configure.py Object path updates
diff_excerpt: source include updates from ftKb_Init.h to ftkirby.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: For path-only renames, preserve existing build classification and section metadata unless the PR also provides evidence for changing them.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Matching/NonMatching classifications retained while paths change
diff_excerpt: splits section start/end ranges retained while paths change

naming_conventions.convention: Kirby TU/header filenames in this PR move from mixed ftKb_* names to lowercase ftkirby/ftkirbyspecial* names, e.g. ftKb_Init.h -> ftkirby.h and ftKb_SpecialNLk.c -> ftkirbyspeciallink.c.
evidence_refs: PR title: Rename Kirby TUs
diff_excerpt: rename from ftKb_Init.h to ftkirby.h
diff_excerpt: rename from ftKb_SpecialNLk.c to ftkirbyspeciallink.c

naming_conventions.convention: The PR spells copied-character Kirby special files by character name rather than abbreviation for the renamed examples: koopa, link, samus, yoshi.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialNKp.c -> ftkirbyspecialkoopa.c
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialNSs.c -> ftkirbyspecialsamus.c

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate TU renames through config, split metadata, and include sites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2602 shows a Kirby TU rename touching split entries, configure.py object paths, renamed C/header/dox files, and broad include-site updates. For similar rename-only work, search all path-bearing surfaces instead of renaming only the source file.

diff_excerpt: configure.py path updates

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting rename-only hygiene for TU path changes
source_update_candidates.text: For rename-only PRs, require coordinated updates to split metadata, build configuration/object lists, renamed headers/docs, and include sites; preserve existing matching status and section ranges unless separate evidence supports changing them.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: splits and configure.py updated with renamed paths
diff_excerpt: source include sites updated to ftkirby.h
source_update_candidates.reason: This may be useful as a general hygiene standard, but one PR with no review discussion is not enough to establish it as accepted global policy without curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all Kirby symbol/function names should be renamed; the diff mainly changes filenames/includes, and many internal identifiers such as ftKb_Init_803CB490 remain unchanged in the shown excerpt.
